source: User accepted local-first standalone UI plan after brainstorming UI should become the CodeWiki product/control room, not only Pi status panel.
summary: Standalone local CodeWiki Control Room UI direction

diff_table.current_state: The primary visual surface is a Pi TUI status panel with compact tabs.
diff_table.desired_state: The primary visual surface becomes a standalone local CodeWiki Control Room that represents CodeWiki entities and can be launched by multiple harnesses.
diff_table.rationale: The UI should be the product experience and should not be constrained by Pi terminal rendering or future harness differences.
diff_table.risk: Larger surface area than the current panel; must keep MVP scoped.

diff_table.desired_state: System view renders architecture.mmd visually with selectable components and an inspector populated from the corresponding system .md file.
diff_table.rationale: Architecture should be navigable and source-backed instead of a static document.
diff_table.risk: Mermaid rendering and component-to-doc mapping must be robust.

diff_table.current_state: Graph tab summarizes counts and rows.
diff_table.desired_state: Graph view provides a visual representation of the generated CodeWiki graph with selectable nodes, filters, and source-backed detail.
diff_table.rationale: Users need to understand relationships and drift visually without reading JSON.
diff_table.risk: Large graphs can overwhelm; MVP needs scoped filtering.

diff_table.current_state: UI styling inherits Pi TUI constraints.
diff_table.desired_state: Standalone UI keeps a retro terminal feel: monospace, dark background, phosphor accents, keyboard-first navigation, command palette, accessible contrast.
diff_table.rationale: Preserves Pi identity while using browser-native visuals.
diff_table.risk: Aesthetic could hurt readability if overdone.

diff_table.desired_state: Default hosting is local-first: a local server serves the UI and CodeWiki API for the current repo; optional shared LAN/server mode can come later with token auth.
diff_table.rationale: Keeps CodeWiki repo-native, offline-capable, and reusable by Pi, Claude Code, Codex, and other harnesses.
diff_table.risk: Multi-machine live collaboration needs careful identity, auth, and conflict semantics; defer beyond MVP.

accepted_decisions.summary: Build a standalone local-first CodeWiki Control Room UI as the primary visual product surface.
Keep Pi TUI as launcher/fallback rather than the long-term rich UI host.
Make System view a rendered architecture diagram plus component inspector sourced from matching system docs.
Make Graph view a visual graph over generated CodeWiki relationships, not a table-only summary.
Use retro terminal styling inspired by Pi while preserving browser accessibility.
Support multiple computers initially through git synchronization and scoped claims; defer real-time shared server mode.

assumptions: The first implementation should be an MVP focused on local browser use.
The UI should read and mutate through CodeWiki API/application capabilities, not Pi internals.
Multi-harness support is a design constraint but Claude/Codex adapters are not part of the first UI slice.
non_goals: No real-time multiplayer collaboration in the first MVP.
No replacement of CodeWiki canonical truth with UI-only state.
